data.content:
### Create Booking API (V2 Example)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Example of creating a booking using the V2 API, including authentication and request body structure.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/bookings

### Description
Create a new booking.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings

### Request Body
- **eventTypeId** (integer) - Required - The ID of the event type.
- **start** (string) - Required - The start time of the booking in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z').
- **attendee** (object) - Required - Information about the attendee.
  - **name** (string) - Required - The attendee's name.
  - **email** (string) - Required - The attendee's email address.
  - **timeZone** (string) - Required - The attendee's time zone (e.g., 'America/New_York').

### Request Example
```json
{
  "eventTypeId": 123,
  "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
  "attendee": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "timeZone": "America/New_York"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the status of the operation (e.g., 'success').
- **data** (object) - Contains the details of the created booking.
  - **id** (integer) - The booking ID.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique booking identifier.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the booking.
  - **startTime** (string) - The start time in ISO 8601 format with milliseconds.
  - **endTime** (string) - The end time in ISO 8601 format with milliseconds.
  - **status** (string) - The booking status (e.g., 'accepted').
  - **attendees** (array) - List of attendees.
  - **metadata** (object) - Additional metadata about the booking.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 123,
    "uid": "cal_abc123xyz",
    "title": "Meeting",
    "startTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
    "endTime": "2024-01-15T11:00:00.000Z",
    "status": "accepted",
    "attendees": [...],
    "metadata": {...}
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get Booking API

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

This endpoint retrieves details for a specific booking using its unique identifier (UID). It can fetch single bookings or all recurrences of a recurring booking.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/bookings/{uid}

### Description
Retrieves booking details by UID, including normal, recurring, or seated bookings.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings/{uid}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **uid** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the booking (e.g., "bk_abc123xyz" for a single booking or "rec_xyz789abc" for recurring bookings).

#### Query Parameters
- **cal-api-version** (string) - Required - The API version to use (e.g., "2024-08-13").

### Request Example
```bash
# Get a single booking
curl -X GET https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings/bk_abc123xyz \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***"

# Get all recurrences of a recurring booking
curl -X GET https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings/rec_xyz789abc \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13"
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - The status of the operation (e.g., "success").
- **data** (object or array) - The booking details. This will be an object for a single booking or an array of objects for recurring booking instances.
  - **id** (integer) - The unique ID of the booking.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique identifier of the booking.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the event.
  - **hosts** (array) - An array of host objects.
    - **id** (integer) - Host's ID.
    - **name** (string) - Host's name.
    - **email** (string) - Host's email.
  - **status** (string) - The status of the booking (e.g., "accepted").
  - **start** (string) - The start time of the booking.
  - **end** (string) - The end time of the booking.
  - **attendees** (array) - An array of attendee objects.
    - **name** (string) - Attendee's name.
    - **email** (string) - Attendee's email.
  - **meetingUrl** (string) - The URL for the meeting.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 456,
    "uid": "bk_abc123xyz",
    "title": "30 Min Meeting",
    "hosts": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Alice Smith",
        "email": "alice@example.com"
      }
    ],
    "status": "accepted",
    "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
    "end": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z",
    "attendees": [
      {
        "name": "John Doe",
        "email": "john@example.com"
      }
    ],
    "meetingUrl": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Booking API

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

This endpoint allows you to create a new booking for an event type. It supports regular, recurring, and instant bookings.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/bookings

### Description
Creates a regular, recurring, or instant booking for an event type.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **cal-api-version** (string) - Required - The API version to use (e.g., "2024-08-13").

#### Request Body
- **start** (string) - Required - The start time of the booking in ISO 8601 format.
- **eventTypeId** (integer) - Required - The ID of the event type.
- **instant** (boolean) - Optional - Set to true for instant bookings.
- **attendee** (object) - Required - Information about the attendee.
  - **name** (string) - Required - Attendee's name.
  - **email** (string) - Required - Attendee's email address.
  - **timeZone** (string) - Required - Attendee's time zone (e.g., "America/New_York").
  - **language** (string) - Optional - Attendee's preferred language.
  - **phoneNumber** (string) - Optional - Attendee's phone number.
- **location** (string) - Optional - The location of the meeting (e.g., "integrations:zoom").
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional metadata for the booking.
  - **notes** (string) - Optional - Notes for the booking.
- **bookingFieldsResponses** (object) - Optional - Custom fields for the booking.
  - **company** (string) - Optional - Attendee's company.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
  "eventTypeId": 123,
  "attendee": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "timeZone": "America/New_York",
    "language": "en",
    "phoneNumber": "+19876543210"
  },
  "location": "integrations:zoom",
  "metadata": {
    "notes": "Please prepare presentation slides"
  },
  "bookingFieldsResponses": {
    "company": "Acme Corp"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - The status of the operation (e.g., "success").
- **data** (object) - The created booking details.
  - **id** (integer) - The unique ID of the booking.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique identifier of the booking.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the event.
  - **description** (string) - The description of the event.
  - **hosts** (array) - An array of host objects.
    - **id** (integer) - Host's ID.
    - **name** (string) - Host's name.
    - **email** (string) - Host's email.
    - **timeZone** (string) - Host's time zone.
  - **status** (string) - The status of the booking (e.g., "accepted").
  - **start** (string) - The start time of the booking.
  - **end** (string) - The end time of the booking.
  - **duration** (integer) - The duration of the booking in minutes.
  - **eventTypeId** (integer) - The ID of the event type.
  - **attendees** (array) - An array of attendee objects.
    - **name** (string) - Attendee's name.
    - **email** (string) - Attendee's email.
    - **timeZone** (string) - Attendee's time zone.
    - **absent** (boolean) - Indicates if the attendee is absent.
  - **location** (string) - The location of the meeting.
  - **meetingUrl** (string) - The URL for the meeting.
  - **absentHost** (boolean) - Indicates if the host is absent.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 456,
    "uid": "bk_abc123xyz",
    "title": "30 Min Meeting",
    "description": "A 30 minute meeting",
    "hosts": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Alice Smith",
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "timeZone": "America/Los_Angeles"
      }
    ],
    "status": "accepted",
    "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
    "end": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z",
    "duration": 30,
    "eventTypeId": 123,
    "attendees": [
      {
        "name": "John Doe",
        "email": "john@example.com",
        "timeZone": "America/New_York",
        "absent": false
      }
    ],
    "location": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789",
    "meetingUrl": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789",
    "absentHost": false
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Enhanced Booking Management API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Endpoints for managing the lifecycle and details of bookings.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/bookings/{uid}/confirm

### Description
Confirm pending bookings.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings/{uid}/confirm

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **uid** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the booking.

## POST /v2/bookings/{uid}/decline

### Description
Decline booking requests.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings/{uid}/decline

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **uid** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the booking.

## POST /v2/bookings/{uid}/mark-absent

### Description
Mark bookings as no-shows.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings/{uid}/mark-absent

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **uid** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the booking.

## POST /v2/bookings/{uid}/reassign

### Description
Reassign bookings to different hosts.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings/{uid}/reassign

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **uid** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the booking.

## POST /v2/bookings/{uid}/guests

### Description
Add guests to existing bookings.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings/{uid}/guests

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **uid** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the booking.
```

### V1 Booking Deletion Example

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Illustrates the V1 endpoint for deleting a booking, which requires the booking ID and API key in the query string.

```bash
DELETE /v1/bookings/123?apiKey=cal_test_xxx
```

--------------------------------

### List Bookings API

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

This endpoint retrieves a list of all bookings for the authenticated user, with support for filtering and pagination.

```APIDOC
## GET /v2/bookings

### Description
Retrieves all bookings for the authenticated user with filtering and pagination.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **cal-api-version** (string) - Required - The API version to use (e.g., "2024-08-13").
- **status** (string) - Optional - Filter bookings by status (e.g., "upcoming", "past").
- **limit** (integer) - Optional - The maximum number of bookings to return (default is usually 10).
- **cursor** (string) - Optional - A cursor for paginating through results.
- **afterStart** (string) - Optional - Filter bookings that start after this ISO 8601 timestamp.
- **beforeEnd** (string) - Optional - Filter bookings that end before this ISO 8601 timestamp.
- **eventTypeIds** (string) - Optional - Filter bookings by a comma-separated list of event type IDs.

### Request Example
```bash
# List bookings with filters
curl -X GET "https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings?status=upcoming&limit=20&cursor=100" \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***"

# Filter by date range and event type
curl -X GET "https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings?afterStart=2024-01-01T00:00:00Z&beforeEnd=2024-01-31T23:59:59Z&eventTypeIds=123,456" \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***"
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - The status of the operation (e.g., "success").
- **data** (array) - An array of booking objects.
  - **id** (integer) - The unique ID of the booking.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique identifier of the booking.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the event.
  - **status** (string) - The status of the booking (e.g., "accepted").
  - **start** (string) - The start time of the booking.
  - **end** (string) - The end time of the booking.
- **pagination** (object) - Pagination details.
  - **nextCursor** (integer) - The cursor for the next page.
  - **prevCursor** (integer) - The cursor for the previous page.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 456,
      "uid": "bk_abc123xyz",
      "title": "30 Min Meeting",
      "status": "accepted",
      "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
      "end": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z"
    },
    {
      "id": 457,
      "uid": "bk_def456uvw",
      "title": "Team Sync",
      "status": "accepted",
      "start": "2024-01-16T14:00:00.000Z",
      "end": "2024-01-16T15:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "nextCursor": 120,
    "prevCursor": 80
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get Booking Calendar Links (API)

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

Retrieves 'Add to Calendar' links for a booking, compatible with major calendar services like Google Calendar, Microsoft Office, and Outlook. It also provides an ICS link. The API requires the booking UID and authentication. The response contains URLs to add the booking event to a user's calendar.

```bash
# Get calendar links for a booking
curl -X GET https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings/bk_abc123xyz/calendar-links \
  -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***"
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v2/bookings - Create a booking

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/v1-v2-differences.mdx

Creates a new booking using the Cal.com API v2. This endpoint has undergone significant changes from v1, particularly in the request body structure.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/bookings

### Description
Creates a new booking for a specified event type and attendee.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/v2/bookings`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **eventTypeId** (integer) - Required - The ID of the event type for the booking.
- **start** (string) - Required - The desired start time for the booking in ISO 8601 format.
- **attendee** (object) - Required - Details of the attendee.
  - **name** (string) - Required - The name of the attendee.
  - **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the attendee.
  - **timeZone** (string) - Required - The time zone of the attendee (e.g., `America/New_York`).
  - **language** (string) - Required - The preferred language for the attendee (e.g., `en`).
- **location** (object) - Required - The location details for the meeting.
  - **type** (string) - Required - The type of meeting location (e.g., `phone`, `video_conference`).
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional key-value pairs for custom data.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "eventTypeId": 123,
  "start": "2024-08-13T09:00:00Z",
  "attendee": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "[email protected]",
    "timeZone": "America/New_York",
    "language": "en"
  },
  "location": {
    "type": "phone"
  },
  "metadata": {}
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the status of the operation (e.g., `success`).
- **data** (object) - Contains the details of the created booking.
  - **id** (integer) - The internal ID of the booking.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique identifier for the booking.
  - **start** (string) - The confirmed start time of the booking.
  - **end** (string) - The confirmed end time of the booking.
  - **duration** (integer) - The duration of the booking in minutes.
  - **status** (string) - The current status of the booking (e.g., `accepted`).
  - **hosts** (array) - List of hosts for the booking.
  - **attendees** (array) - List of attendees for the booking.
  - **eventType** (object) - Details about the event type.
    - **id** (integer) - The ID of the event type.
    - **slug** (string) - The slug of the event type.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 123,
    "uid": "booking_uid_123",
    "start": "2024-08-13T15:30:00Z",
    "end": "2024-08-13T16:30:00Z",
    "duration": 60,
    "status": "accepted",
    "hosts": [],
    "attendees": [],
    "eventType": {
      "id": 1,
      "slug": "some-event"
    }
  }
}
```
```
